```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

library(tidyverse)
library(brms)
library(bayesplot)
library(loo)
library(posterior)
library(skimr)
library(knitr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggdist)
library(tidybayes)

# plotting theme
theme_set(theme_minimal())

# use parallel computing if possible
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
```

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

# Load your plane crash data
crashes <- read_csv("data/aircrashdata.csv")

# Clean column names and filter valid observations
crashes <- crashes %>%
  rename(
    year = Year,
    quarter = Quarter,
    month = Month,
    day = Day,
    country = `Country/Region`,
    aircraft_manufacturer = `Aircraft Manufacturer`,
    aircraft_type = Aircraft,
    location = Location,
    operator = Operator,
    ground = Ground,
    fatalities = `Fatalities (air)`,
    aboard = Aboard
  ) %>%
  # Filter valid observations
  filter(
    !is.na(fatalities),
    !is.na(aboard),
    !is.na(aircraft_type),
    aboard > 0,
    fatalities <= aboard  # Sanity check: can't have more deaths than people aboard
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate fatality rate once
    fatality_rate = fatalities / aboard,
    # Standardize year for better convergence
    year_scaled = scale(year)[,1],
  )
  
```

## Overview

```{r}
#| label: data-summary

# overview statistics
skim_with(
  character = sfl(min = NULL, max = NULL, empty = NULL, whitespace = NULL),
  numeric = sfl(hist = NULL)
)(crashes)

# first rows of dataset
head(crashes) %>% 
  kable(caption = "First few rows of plane crash data")
```

## Crashes Over Time

```{r}
#| label: num-of-crashes
#| fig-cap: "Temporal trends in aviation accidents"
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 4

# Plot with just the line (data points)
p1 <- crashes %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(n_crashes = n(),
            total_fatalities = sum(fatalities, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = n_crashes)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Number of Crashes Over Time (Data)",
       x = "Year", y = "Number of Crashes")

# Plot with just the trend line
p2 <- crashes %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(n_crashes = n(),
            total_fatalities = sum(fatalities, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = n_crashes)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Number of Crashes Over Time (Trend)",
       x = "Year", y = "Number of Crashes")

# Display all four plots in a 2x2 grid
(p1 | p2)
```

## Fatalities Over Time

```{r}
#| label: fatalities-over-time
#| fig-cap: "Trends in aviation accidents"
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 4

# Plot with just data points
p1 <- crashes %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = fatalities)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Fatalities Over Time (Data Points)",
       x = "Year", y = "Fatalities")

# Plot with just the trend line
p2 <- crashes %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = fatalities)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Fatalities Over Time (Trend)",
       x = "Year", y = "Fatalities")

(p1 | p2)

```


## Fatality Rate Over Time

```{r}
#| label: fatality-rate
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 4

# Plot 1: Fatality rate over time
p1 <- crashes %>%
  mutate(fatality_rate = fatalities / aboard) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = fatality_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", color = "blue", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Fatality Rate Over Time",
       x = "Year", y = "Fatality Rate (proportion)") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)

# Plot 2: Distribution of fatalities
p2 <- crashes %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fatalities)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Fatalities per Crash",
       x = "Fatalities", y = "Count")

# Combine plots
(p1 | p2)
```
